sections.paragraph:
fut. יֶאֱגֹר.-

(1) to collect, to gather in (the harvest), Deu 28:39 Pro 6:8 10:5 comp. גּוּר No. 2, and יְגַר, also Gr. ἀγείρω. (Prob. this root has the signification, to scrape together, comp. the kindred גָּרַר. By softening the roughness of the letter ר we have אָגַל and גָּלַל, which are used of the rolling and confluence of water.)

(2) In the cognate languages the signification of gain and profit is found (from the idea of scraping together), also of wages as arising from that of gain; see אִגֶּרֶת . Other derivatives are אָגוּר, אֲגֹורָה, according to most אֲגַרְטָל.
